issues.title: Admin REST client logic duplicated between apps/admin and apps/payments
issues.duplicatedCode: Both files independently define the same Supabase admin REST plumbing: reading SUPABASE_URL_INTERNAL/NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, building service-role headers (apikey + Authorization + Content-Type), constructing the /rest/v1/<path> URL, throwing on missing config, and a generic fetch-with-error-check wrapper (adminFetch / adminFetchJson). createRestPath and validateUuid are also copied verbatim between the two files.
issues.description: apps/admin/src/app/api/admin/_shared/adminRest.ts and apps/payments/src/shared/infrastructure/adminRestClient.ts both implement: (1) env-var read for Supabase URL + service-role key; (2) getRestHeaders / getAdminRestHeaders – identical header object; (3) getRestUrl / getAdminRestUrl – identical URL construction; (4) adminFetch / adminFetchJson – nearly identical fetch + error-check pattern; (5) createRestPath – identical URLSearchParams builder (both files); (6) validateUuid – nearly identical UUID regex check (different regex variant but same intent). Any change to the Supabase REST layer (header name, error format, URL pattern) must be made in two places.
issues.fix: Extract the shared primitives (env-var reads, getAdminRestHeaders, getAdminRestUrl, adminFetchJson, createRestPath, validateUuid) into a new server-side utility in packages/shared or a new app-level shared module. Both admin and payments Route Handlers import from there. Each app can keep its own higher-level helpers (fetchGrantedPermissions, getAuthorizedAdmin, etc.) that call the shared primitives.

issues.title: useSellerProfiles hook duplicated in store and payments with inlined query logic
issues.duplicatedCode: Both hooks fetch user_profiles rows by a list of seller IDs and build a Record<sellerId, displayName> map. The store hook inlines the Supabase query; payments delegates to fetchSellerProfiles() which does the same query with the same field selection (id, display_name, email). The query key ("seller-profiles") is also duplicated as a raw string literal.
issues.description: apps/store/src/features/cart/application/hooks/useSellerProfiles.ts and apps/payments/src/features/checkout/application/hooks/useSellerProfiles.ts are near-identical hooks (same query key string, same Supabase table/columns, same map-building logic). The only differences are: (a) store deduplicates IDs with [...new Set(...)], payments does not; (b) store falls back to email.split('@')[0], payments app uses FALLBACK_SELLER_NAME constant. There is also a third implementation of the same query inside fetchUserDisplayNames.ts in payments shared infrastructure that does the same SELECT with a fallbackName parameter.
issues.fix: Promote the shared Supabase query to payments/src/shared/infrastructure/fetchUserDisplayNames.ts (already exists) and have the store hook import from there via a dedicated infrastructure module. Alternatively, extract a shared useSellerProfiles hook into packages/shared (if the supabase client pattern supports it). At minimum, remove the inline query from store's hook and have it call a dedicated fetchSellerProfiles function just like the payments hook does.

issues.title: PRODUCT_TYPES array constant defined independently in store and studio with different shapes
issues.duplicatedCode: Both files define a PRODUCT_TYPES constant listing the same four product type values (merch, digital, service, ticket). Store uses { value: ProductType }[] while studio uses ProductType[]. Additionally, studio independently defines PRODUCT_CATEGORIES: ProductCategory[] with the same six values already present in packages/shared as PRODUCT_CATEGORIES: { value; color }[].
issues.description: The canonical set of product types (merch | digital | service | ticket) is defined as a union type in packages/shared/src/types/product.ts but there is no exported runtime array constant in shared. Both apps therefore roll their own lists. If a new product type is added to the DB enum, it must be updated in three places: the shared type union, the store constant, and the studio constant. The PRODUCT_CATEGORIES case is similar but studio duplicates just the values (not the color metadata) from the shared package.
issues.fix: Add a PRODUCT_TYPES: ProductType[] constant to packages/shared/src/utils/ (analogous to the existing PRODUCT_CATEGORIES array) and export it from the shared index. Both store and studio import from shared. The store wrapper constant { value: ProductType } can be built locally from the shared array if needed. For studio's PRODUCT_CATEGORIES, remove the local definition and import from shared (the simple ProductCategory[] values are already available as .map(c => c.value) from the shared object array).

issues.title: catalog and products searchParams are near-identical across store and studio
issues.duplicatedCode: Both files export a nuqs searchParams object with the exact same three fields (category, type, q) all using parseAsString.withDefault(""). The only difference is the export name (catalogSearchParams vs productsSearchParams) and field ordering.
issues.description: apps/store/src/features/products/domain/searchParams.ts exports { category, type, q } and apps/studio/src/features/products/domain/searchParams.ts exports the exact same three parsers with the same defaults. These represent the same knowledge: the URL filter params for product list views. A new filter field added to the product query (e.g. a 'featured' flag) needs to be added to both files.
issues.fix: Per KISS: evaluate whether both apps will always share the same filter surface. If yes, extract a shared productFilterSearchParams object to packages/shared/src/utils/ or a new shared domain module, and re-export with local aliases in each app. If the two apps are expected to diverge (e.g. studio may add admin-only filters), keep them separate and accept the duplication — this is coincidental duplication that may evolve differently.

patterns.description: Re-export pattern is used consistently and correctly. All useSupabaseAuth hooks, environment config, api config, category constants, and SupabaseClient type are thin re-exports from shared packages — no duplication of logic.
PaymentMethodTypes are properly promoted to a shared layer (apps/payments/src/shared/domain/PaymentMethodTypes.ts) with re-exports from the feature domain layer — no duplication.

formatCop is already extracted to packages/shared and re-exported through apps/payments/src/shared/application/utils/formatCop.ts. Inline toLocaleString COP formatting in CheckoutItemsSummary.tsx bypasses this utility (2 occurrences in same component).
patterns.assessment: minor — within single component, not cross-app duplication
